   "source": [
    "# Install gwexpy with pinned versions of core dependencies for reproducibility on Colab\n",
    "\n",
    "%pip install -q \"gwexpy[all]\" \"gwpy<5.0.0\" \"numpy<2.0.0\" \"scipy<1.13.0\" \"astropy<7.0.0\"\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "f8522d2c",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Wiener Filtering: Coherent Noise Subtraction\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/case_wiener_filter.ipynb)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "479ab361",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "A multichannel Wiener filter is useful when several witness sensors contain **overlapping views of the same physical noise sources**. Treating those witnesses independently can double-count the noise and over-subtract the target.\n",
    "\n",
    "This compact public case study promotes the legacy MIMO notebook and focuses on the core matrix workflow."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "61a2f52c",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-20T16:31:13.120296Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-20T16:31:13.120140Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-20T16:31:16.415169Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-20T16:31:16.413903Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from scipy import signal\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy import FrequencySeriesDict, TimeSeries, TimeSeriesDict\n",
    "from gwexpy.noise.asd import from_pygwinc\n",
    "from gwexpy.noise.wave import from_asd\n",
    "\n",
    "fs = 2048.0\n",
    "duration = 64.0\n",
    "t = np.arange(0, duration, 1 / fs)\n",
    "n = len(t)\n",
    "np.random.seed(123)\n",
    "rng = np.random.default_rng(123)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Two independent physical sources are mixed into two witnesses with different weights, creating crosstalk between sensors.\n",
    "b_low, a_low = signal.butter(2, 5.0, fs=fs, btype=\"low\")\n",
    "s1 = signal.lfilter(b_low, a_low, np.random.normal(0, 10.0, n))\n",
    "b_band, a_band = signal.iirpeak(120.0, 30.0, fs=fs)\n",
    "s2 = signal.lfilter(b_band, a_band, np.random.normal(0, 2.0, n)) + np.random.normal(0, 0.1, n)\n",
    "aux1_val = 1.0 * s1 + 0.4 * s2 + np.random.normal(0, 0.05, n)\n",
    "aux2_val = 0.6 * s1 + 1.0 * s2 + np.random.normal(0, 0.05, n)\n",
    "\n",
    "asd_main = from_pygwinc(\"aLIGO\", fmin=5.0, fmax=fs / 2, df=1.0 / duration, quantity=\"strain\")\n",
    "tsd = TimeSeriesDict()\n",
    "tsd[\"MAIN\"] = from_asd(asd_main, duration, fs, t0=0, rng=rng).highpass(5.0)\n",
    "tsd[\"AUX1\"] = TimeSeries(aux1_val, sample_rate=fs, unit=\"V\")\n",
    "tsd[\"AUX2\"] = TimeSeries(aux2_val, sample_rate=fs, unit=\"V\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Add witness-coupled noise into MAIN so subtraction has a coherent target to remove.\n",
    "tsd[\"MAIN\"] += tsd[\"AUX1\"] / tsd[\"AUX1\"].unit * tsd[\"MAIN\"].unit * 2e-22\n",
    "tsd[\"MAIN\"] += tsd[\"AUX2\"] / tsd[\"AUX2\"].unit * tsd[\"MAIN\"].unit * 5e-22\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "7f02921e",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. Estimate the multichannel correlation matrices\n",
    "\n",
    "The key point is that the witnesses are not independent. The `Cxx` matrix captures witness-to-witness correlation, and `Cyx` captures how the target correlates with those witnesses."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "18b3fc59",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-20T16:31:16.418445Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-20T16:31:16.418062Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-20T16:31:17.294392Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-20T16:31:17.293649Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Plot size 1800x500 with 2 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "aux_names = [\"AUX1\", \"AUX2\"]\n",
    "aux_tsd = TimeSeriesDict({k: tsd[k] for k in aux_names})\n",
    "\n",
    "# Window-averaged CSD matrices stabilize the statistics before we invert the witness correlation matrix.\n",
    "cxx = aux_tsd.csd_matrix(fftlength=8.0)\n",
    "cyx = TimeSeriesDict({\"MAIN\": tsd[\"MAIN\"]}).csd_matrix(other=aux_tsd, fftlength=8.0)\n",
    "\n",
    "# H = Cyx @ Cxx^-1 is the multichannel Wiener solution: it predicts the coherent part of MAIN from the witness set.\n",
    "H_lowres = cyx @ cxx.inv()\n",
    "H_lowres.abs().plot(xscale=\"log\", yscale=\"log\").suptitle(\"Estimated MIMO Coupling (H)\")\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "0d624580",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Project and subtract the coherent noise\n",
    "\n",
    "Only the coherent, witness-predictable part should be removed. Irreducible target noise should remain."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "fea1c8e4",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-20T16:31:17.296334Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-20T16:31:17.296116Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-20T16:31:17.654595Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-20T16:31:17.653486Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1000x600 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "tsd_fft = tsd.fft()\n",
    "H = H_lowres.interpolate(tsd_fft[\"MAIN\"].frequencies)\n",
    "X_mat = FrequencySeriesDict({k: tsd_fft[k] for k in aux_names}).to_matrix()\n",
    "\n",
    "# Projection estimates the part of MAIN that can be reconstructed from the witnesses.\n",
    "Y_proj = (H @ X_mat)[0, 0]\n",
    "cleaned_ts = tsd[\"MAIN\"] - Y_proj.ifft().bandpass(100, 130).real\n",
    "\n",
    "asd_raw = tsd[\"MAIN\"].asd(fftlength=4.0)\n",
    "asd_cleaned = cleaned_ts.asd(fftlength=4.0)\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
    "plt.loglog(asd_raw, label=\"Original Main\")\n",
    "plt.loglog(asd_cleaned, label=\"Cleaned\")\n",
    "plt.xlim(10, 1000)\n",
    "plt.legend()\n",
    "plt.grid(True, which=\"both\")\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  }
 ],
